page: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring 16 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC-Rome processors built on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz with 16 physical cores and a single thread per core configuration under a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 3840 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. Multi-core performance, measured via stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is also average, delivering 123.94 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 70.41 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database operations, evaluated through Redis SET benchmarks, achieve an average throughput of 28.04 million operations per second.

Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and substantial local NVMe SSD capacity, this instance offers a balanced resource density for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads without the premium of dedicated CPU allocations. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained peak loads, which is a key tradeoff to consider. It is suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, static web serving, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: The Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC-Rome processors on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, the instance provides 128.0 GB of system memory, equating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes a bundled 3840 GB local NVMe SSD and one complimentary public IPv4 address. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. With its 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-capacity local storage, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy and storage-centric workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and static web serving, though the shared CPU model presents a tradeoff for sustained high-compute tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-16c-128gb-3840s-amd features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual server combining a high-capacity NVMe SSD with generous memory allocation for database and memory-intensive workloads.
